_comment: Upstream originals the pack can fetch at install time. Do not replace these with ASP-authored summaries. Read each upstream README before install/update. The commit field is a last-reviewed reference, not a required install target. Licenses and notices must be copied alongside each installed component (see VENDORING.md).

sources.name: caveman
sources.author: Julius Brussee (JuliusBrussee)
sources.note: Canonical caveman skill. Read the upstream README first. For agent-side/defaults setup, treat Caveman as a pure skill that defaults to the active host's agent-side skill location, and tell the user before broad agent config changes. For project-level setup, use upstream project-local options when available or ask before broader host changes. Use file/commit as last-reviewed location hints only.

sources.name: stop-slop
sources.author: Hardik Pandya (hardikpandya)
sources.note: Removes AI writing tells from prose. No bundled ASP rewrite — read the upstream README first, then copy only the upstream SKILL.md + required references as-is when the README calls for file copying. Stop Slop works as either project-level or agent-side/defaults skill; default to agent-side placement for defaults setup and use project-level placement when the user chooses project setup.

sources.name: mattpocock/skills
sources.author: Matt Pocock (mattpocock)
sources.note: A collection of production engineering skills (TDD, architecture review, planning, git workflow, etc.). Read the upstream README first. As of the last review, upstream recommends `npx skills add mattpocock/skills`; use that installer. For agent-side/defaults setup, explain that ASP intentionally installs reusable pure skills into the active agent's own skill area, then add `-g/--global` or choose Global if that is the upstream flag/prompt. For project setup, do not add `-g/--global`, choose Project if prompted for scope, let the user pick sub-skills or all, and include setup-matt-pocock-skills when upstream recommends it. Special case: setup-matt-pocock-skills may be available from the agent-side skill area as a trigger, but its work is per-project; running it in one repo does not configure every repo. Copy folders manually only when upstream no longer provides a usable installer path.
